key_insight: Spacer-layer Cl doping can tune low-n square-planar nickelates to a Ni valence and correlation regime similar to superconducting higher-n compounds, making n=2 and n=3 candidates for superconductivity.
engineering_relevance: This provides a chemically practical design route to engineer nickelate superconductors by adjusting spacer-layer composition rather than relying only on difficult control of layer number.
method: Used DFT+DMFT to model layer-resolved electronic structure and Ni-d orbital correlations.
Compared electron-compensated La-based n=2 and n=3 nickelates against superconducting higher-n nickelates.
Evaluated whether Cl substitution preserves the low-energy Ni-d electronic structure while shifting valence.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical evaluation showing that Cl-doped n=2 and n=3 compounds reach Ni-d correlations comparable to superconducting higher-n members while retaining the characteristic low-energy electronic structure.
watch_item: The result is a prediction from electronic-structure calculations, so the proposed superconductivity still requires experimental synthesis and validation, including control of Cl incorporation and structural stability.
paper_title: Layer-resolved Electronic Structure and Correlation of Low-$n$ Square-planar Nickelates: A DFT+DMFT Prediction of Superconducting Candidates
